batches.title: Mediterranean Grilled Chicken, Roasted Vegetables & Quinoa
steps: Season 650g chicken breast with dried oregano, lemon juice, minced garlic, salt, pepper
Grill or bake chicken at 200°C for 18 min until cooked through; cool and portion into 2 servings
Chop 200g bell peppers, 200g zucchini, 100g carrots; toss with 1.5 tbsp olive oil, 1 tsp paprika, 1 tsp thyme
Roast vegetables at 200°C for 22 min until tender; cool and portion into 2 servings
Cook 150g dry quinoa in 300ml vegetable stock; cool and portion into 2 servings
Store all components in separate sealed containers in fridge
yields: Grilled chicken: 2 portions (~180g Diego, 140g Diana)
Roasted vegetables: 2 portions (~200g Diego, 160g Diana)
Cooked quinoa: 2 portions (~90g Diego, 70g Diana)

day_of_assembly.Monday: Lunch — Mediterranean Grilled Chicken with Roasted Vegetables & Quinoa: reheat 180g chicken + quinoa, add roasted veg, drizzle 1 tsp olive oil
day_of_assembly.Wednesday: Dinner — Grilled Chicken Breast with Couscous, Roasted Peppers & Spinach: do not use this batch (fresh couscous dinner on Wed)

storage.safety_note: Cool quickly; never leave poultry at room temp > 2 h. Reheat to 74 °C through.

batches.title: Asian Chicken Stir-Fry with Bok Choy, Carrots & Brown Rice
steps: Cut 600g chicken breast into bite-sized pieces; season with salt, pepper, minced garlic
Stir-fry chicken in 1.5 tbsp olive oil over high heat for 8 min until cooked; cool and portion
Slice 500g bok choy lengthwise; julienne 200g carrots
Heat 1 tbsp sesame oil in wok; stir-fry bok choy and carrots for 4 min; season with 2 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tsp minced ginger; cool and portion
Cook 120g dry brown rice in 240ml water; cool and portion into 2 servings
Store chicken, stir-fried vegetables, and rice in separate sealed containers
yields: Cooked chicken pieces: 2 portions (~160g Diego, 130g Diana)
Stir-fried bok choy & carrots: 2 portions (~180g Diego, 150g Diana)
Cooked brown rice: 2 portions (~80g Diego, 65g Diana)

day_of_assembly.Tuesday: Lunch — Asian Chicken Stir-Fry with Bok Choy, Sesame & Brown Rice: reheat chicken 2 min, add reheated vegetables, top cooked rice, drizzle 0.5 tsp sesame oil, sprinkle sesame seeds

storage.safety_note: Cool quickly; never leave poultry at room temp > 2 h. Reheat to 74 °C through.

batches.title: Baked White Fish, Green Beans, Mushrooms & New Potatoes
steps: Boil 400g new potatoes with skin until tender (~18 min); cool and portion into 2 servings
Steam 500g green beans for 8 min until tender-crisp; cool and portion into 2 servings
Sauté 300g diced mushrooms in 1 tbsp olive oil with minced garlic and 0.5 tsp thyme for 6 min; cool and portion into 2 servings
Store each component in separate sealed containers in fridge
yields: Boiled new potatoes: 2 portions (~100g each)
Steamed green beans: 2 portions (~120g Diego, 100g Diana)
Sautéed mushrooms: 2 portions (~75g Diego, 60g Diana)

day_of_assembly.Monday: Dinner — Baked White Fish with Green Beans, Mushrooms & New Potatoes: bake 160g (Diego) or 130g (Diana) fresh fish 10 min, reheat potatoes/beans/mushrooms, plate and drizzle olive oil
day_of_assembly.Friday: ⚠ Cook fresh today — do NOT use the Sunday batch (vegetables cooked is only safe ~4 day(s); this is day 5).
day_of_assembly.Sunday: ⚠ Cook fresh today — do NOT use the Sunday batch (vegetables cooked is only safe ~4 day(s); this is day 7).

storage.safety_note: Cooked vegetables store well. Roasted veg can be eaten cold in salads.

batches.title: Lean Beef & Herb Meatballs with Tomato Sauce & Whole-Grain Pasta
steps: Mix 550g lean beef mince with 30g grated onion (raw), 1 large egg, minced garlic, 1 tsp dried oregano, salt, pepper
Roll into 16 golf-ball-sized meatballs; place on baking tray; bake at 180°C for 14 min until cooked; cool and portion (8 balls per person × 2 = 16 total)
Sauté minced garlic in 1 tbsp olive oil; add 400g tomato paste + 200ml water; simmer 8 min with 1 tsp dried basil and 0.5 tsp oregano; cool and portion
Cook 200g dry whole-grain pasta al dente (~10 min); cool and portion into 2 servings
Store meatballs, sauce, and pasta in separate sealed containers
yields: Cooked beef meatballs: 16 pieces (~5 per Diego serving, 4 per Diana serving)
Tomato sauce: 2 portions (~120g Diego, 100g Diana)
Cooked whole-grain pasta: 2 portions (~100g Diego, 80g Diana)

day_of_assembly.Tuesday: Dinner — Lean Beef & Herb Meatballs with Tomato Sauce & Whole-Grain Pasta: reheat 100g (Diego) or 80g (Diana) pasta, reheat 5 (Diego) or 4 (Diana) meatballs + sauce, plate and garnish with basil

storage.safety_note: Store in coldest part of fridge. Reheat to 74 °C before serving.

batches.title: Grilled Chicken Breast with Couscous, Roasted Peppers & Spinach
steps: Season 600g chicken breast with 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp paprika, minced garlic, salt, pepper
Grill or bake chicken at 200°C for 18 min until cooked through; cool and portion into 2 servings
Chop 400g bell peppers (red & yellow); toss with 1 tbsp olive oil, 0.5 tsp cumin, thyme
Roast peppers at 200°C for 15 min; cool and portion into 2 servings
Cook 120g dry couscous in 240ml vegetable stock; fluff; cool and portion into 2 servings
Sauté 300g fresh spinach in 0.5 tbsp olive oil with minced garlic for 3 min until wilted; cool and portion into 2 servings
Store all components separately in sealed containers
yields: Grilled chicken breast: 2 portions (~170g Diego, 140g Diana)
Roasted bell peppers: 2 portions (~120g Diego, 100g Diana)
Cooked couscous: 2 portions (~75g Diego, 60g Diana)
Wilted spinach: 2 portions (~100g Diego, 80g Diana)

day_of_assembly.Wednesday: Dinner — Grilled Chicken Breast with Couscous, Roasted Peppers & Spinach: reheat 170g (Diego) or 140g (Diana) chicken, reheat peppers and spinach, portion couscous, plate together and drizzle 0.5 tsp olive oil

storage.safety_note: Cool quickly; never leave poultry at room temp > 2 h. Reheat to 74 °C through.

batches.title: Soft-Boiled Eggs (for 5 breakfasts)
steps: Bring large pot of water to rolling boil
Gently lower 10 large eggs into boiling water
Cook for exactly 6 minutes for soft-boiled consistency (runny yolk, set white)
Transfer immediately to ice bath to stop cooking
Once cool, gently peel under cool running water, being careful with soft yolk
Store in sealed container with paper towel to absorb moisture; keep refrigerated
yields: 10 soft-boiled eggs, peeled and stored (used for Monday, Wednesday, Thursday, Saturday, Sunday breakfasts)

day_of_assembly.Monday: Breakfast — Soft-Boiled Egg with Whole-Grain Sourdough, Feta & Strawberries: toast bread, place 1 egg (Diego) or 0.75 egg (Diana) in cup, crumble feta on toast, arrange strawberries
day_of_assembly.Wednesday: Breakfast — Soft-Boiled Egg with Whole-Grain Sourdough, Feta & Blueberries: toast bread, place 1 egg (Diego) or 0.75 egg (Diana) in cup, crumble feta on toast, arrange blueberries
day_of_assembly.Thursday: Breakfast — Soft-Boiled Egg with Whole-Grain Sourdough, Feta & Strawberries: toast bread, place 1 egg (Diego) or 0.75 egg (Diana) in cup, crumble feta on toast, arrange strawberries
day_of_assembly.Saturday: ⚠ Cook fresh today — do NOT use the Sunday batch (eggs cooked is only safe ~4 day(s); this is day 6).
day_of_assembly.Sunday: ⚠ Cook fresh today — do NOT use the Sunday batch (eggs cooked is only safe ~4 day(s); this is day 7).

storage.safety_note: Hard-boiled eggs: 1 week in shell, 5 days peeled in water. Scrambled/cooked dishes: 3–4 days.

batches.title: Mexican-Style Chicken & Black Bean Bowl with Cilantro-Lime Rice
steps: Season 600g chicken breast with 1.5 tsp chili powder, 1 tsp cumin, 0.5 tsp paprika, minced garlic, salt, pepper
Bake chicken at 180°C for 18 min until cooked; cool and shred with forks; portion into 2 servings
Cook 150g dry brown rice in 300ml water; stir in juice of 1 lime and 2 tbsp fresh chopped cilantro; cool and portion into 2 servings
Roast 400g diced bell peppers with 1 tbsp olive oil, 1 tsp cumin, 0.5 tsp chili powder at 200°C for 14 min; cool and portion into 2 servings
Heat 1 can (400g) black beans with minced garlic and 0.5 tsp cumin for 5 min; cool and portion into 2 servings
Store each component separately in sealed containers
yields: Shredded cooked chicken: 2 portions (~160g Diego, 130g Diana)
Cilantro-lime rice: 2 portions (~85g Diego, 68g Diana)
Roasted bell peppers: 2 portions (~110g Diego, 90g Diana)
Heated black beans: 2 portions (~90g Diego, 70g Diana)

day_of_assembly.Thursday: ⚠ Cook fresh today — do NOT use the Sunday batch (poultry is only safe ~3 day(s); this is day 4).

storage.safety_note: Cool quickly; never leave poultry at room temp > 2 h. Reheat to 74 °C through.

batches.title: Tuna & White Bean Salad Components
steps: Open 3 cans (750g total) white beans; drain and rinse thoroughly; divide into 2 portions and store in sealed containers
Dice 2 red/yellow bell peppers (350g total); dice 1 large cucumber (300g total)
Whisk together 1 tbsp balsamic vinegar + 1 tsp olive oil; toss with diced peppers and cucumber; divide into 2 portions and store in sealed containers
Wash and dry 200g fresh arugula; store in airtight container with paper towel to absorb moisture
yields: Cooked white beans: 2 portions (~80g Diego, 65g Diana)
Mixed diced bell pepper & cucumber with balsamic: 2 portions (~100g Diego, 80g Diana)
Fresh arugula: 200g total (50g Diego, 40g Diana per serving)

day_of_assembly.Thursday: Dinner — Tuna & White Bean Salad: arrange 50g (Diego) or 40g (Diana) fresh arugula as base, flake 1 can (100g Diego) or 0.75 can (75g Diana) fresh tuna on top, add beans and dressed vegetables, drizzle 1 tsp (Diego) or 0.7 tsp (Diana) olive oil

storage.safety_note: Pre-cut vegetables: store dry, use within 3–4 days. Wash just before eating.

batches.title: Harissa-Spiced Chicken with Roasted Vegetables & Chickpeas
steps: Season 600g chicken breast with 2 tbsp harissa paste, juice of 0.5 lemon, minced garlic, salt, pepper
Grill or bake chicken at 200°C for 18 min until cooked through; cool and portion into 2 servings
Chop 300g carrots; chop 300g zucchini
Drain 1 can (400g) chickpeas; rinse
Toss carrots, zucchini, chickpeas with 1.5 tbsp olive oil, 0.5 tsp harissa, 1 tsp ground coriander
Roast all together at 200°C for 18 min until vegetables are tender; cool and portion into 2 servings
Store chicken and roasted vegetable-chickpea mix in separate sealed containers
yields: Harissa-spiced grilled chicken: 2 portions (~170g Diego, 140g Diana)
Roasted carrots, zucchini & chickpeas: 2 portions (~230g Diego, ~185g Diana)

day_of_assembly.Friday: ⚠ Cook fresh today — do NOT use the Sunday batch (poultry is only safe ~3 day(s); this is day 5).

storage.safety_note: Cool quickly; never leave poultry at room temp > 2 h. Reheat to 74 °C through.

batches.title: Baked White Fish Accompaniments: Broccoli, Cherry Tomatoes & Potatoes
steps: Boil 380g new potatoes with skin until tender (~16 min); cool and portion into 2 servings
Chop 450g fresh broccoli into florets; toss with 1 tbsp olive oil, minced garlic, 0.5 tsp thyme
Roast broccoli at 200°C for 14 min until golden; cool and portion into 2 servings
Halve 300g cherry tomatoes; toss with 0.5 tbsp olive oil, 0.5 tsp dried oregano
Roast tomatoes alongside broccoli for final 8 min; cool and portion into 2 servings
Store potatoes, broccoli, and tomatoes in separate sealed containers
yields: Boiled new potatoes: 2 portions (~100g each)
Roasted broccoli florets: 2 portions (~130g Diego, 110g Diana)
Roasted cherry tomatoes: 2 portions (~80g Diego, 65g Diana)

day_of_assembly.Friday: ⚠ Cook fresh today — do NOT use the Sunday batch (vegetables cooked is only safe ~4 day(s); this is day 5).

storage.safety_note: Cooked vegetables store well. Roasted veg can be eaten cold in salads.

batches.title: Grilled Lean Beef with Roasted Eggplant & Mushrooms, Couscous
steps: Season 500g lean beef steak (sirloin or rump) with salt, pepper, minced garlic, 0.5 tsp thyme
Grill or pan-sear beef over high heat 4 min per side for medium; cool; slice into 2 portions
Slice 350g eggplant lengthwise into 0.5 cm planks; dice 300g mushrooms
Toss eggplant and mushrooms with 1.5 tbsp olive oil, 1 tsp dried oregano, minced garlic
Roast at 200°C for 16 min until tender; cool and portion into 2 servings
Cook 100g dry couscous in 200ml vegetable stock; fluff; cool and portion into 2 servings
Store beef, roasted vegetables, and couscous in separate sealed containers
yields: Grilled lean beef slices: 2 portions (~140g Diego, 110g Diana)
Roasted eggplant & mushrooms: 2 portions (~180g Diego, 150g Diana)
Cooked couscous: 2 portions (~65g Diego, 52g Diana)

day_of_assembly.Saturday: ⚠ Cook fresh today — do NOT use the Sunday batch (red meat is only safe ~3 day(s); this is day 6).

storage.safety_note: Store in coldest part of fridge. Reheat to 74 °C before serving.
